description: Cross-language conformance for DISCRETE-CADENCE MATERIALIZATION — the middle cadence phase (const ⊏ discrete ⊏ continuous; CONFORMANCE_SPEC.md §5.13). A state-free array observed that mixes a CONST weight (`M.W`) with a DISCRETE forcing field (`M.src`) inside a contraction `g[j] = sum_i W[i,j]*src[i]` changes ONLY when the forcing is refreshed at a cadence boundary: a DISCRETE field a materializing binding caches once per refresh and gathers into the hot RHS, while a sibling `k[j] = sum_i W[i,j]*offset` reads only const/parameter data and stays CONST-cadence (build-once/inline). Each binding drives a segmented solve of `D(c[j]) = g[j] + k[j]`, writing `src` from the golden's `forcing.by_anchor` snapshots at `refresh_times` and threading state across segments; the piecewise-constant forcing makes the trajectory analytic and exact. Complements `tests/conformance/build_once_spatial_field/` (the CONST tier of the same materialize⇄gather seam, no refresh) and `tests/conformance/refresh/` (the full loader→regrid→refresh capstone). This set isolates the const-vs-discrete cadence classification inside a contraction, with NO regrid (src delivered directly on the sim index) and NO providers (offline snapshots driven by the adapter).
